course_description.overview: A timed cyclosportive (non-competitive personal challenge) through the Charlevoix backcountry featuring two distance options: 83 km and 47 km. The event emphasizes personal achievement over competition, with no podium or competitive pressure.

key_segments.name: Charlevoix Backcountry Routes
key_segments.description: Carefully designed road courses showcasing the Charlevoix territory's striking landscapes

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The event is described as demanding yet accessible, designed to challenge both mind and legs. Specific elevation profiles and technical details for 2026 are not yet finalized.

logistics.getting_there: Located in Baie-Saint-Paul, Quebec, Canada. Free on-site parking available.
logistics.registration_details: Online registration only. Details regarding course specifics, services, and registration opening for 2026 will be announced in coming months.
logistics.bike_logistics: Mandatory equipment: short or long-sleeved jerseys covering shoulders
logistics.local_considerations: Event is certified ÉcoFQSC Argent (environmental certification). Participants encouraged to carpool, bring reusable water bottles (water stations available on-site), and participate in waste sorting (recycling, compost, and special waste bins provided).

final_verdict.one_liner: A scenic, accessible cyclosportive through Quebec's Charlevoix region offering personal challenge without competitive pressure in a UNESCO biosphere reserve setting.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking a personal challenge in stunning scenery; recreational riders wanting a structured event without podium pressure; cyclists interested in exploring the Charlevoix backcountry; environmentally-conscious participants.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those prioritizing scenic beauty and personal achievement over competitive racing. The event's emphasis on experience and environmental stewardship adds value beyond the cycling itself.
pro_tips: Register online when registration opens—on-site registration is no longer permitted
Bring reusable water bottles to take advantage of on-site water stations and support the event's environmental certification
Plan accommodation early as Baie-Saint-Paul is a popular tourist destination during late May
Monitor velocharlevoix.ca for 2026 course details, as full route information is still being finalized

youtube_data.search_query: Granfondo Charlevoix gran fondo cycling Baie-Saint-Paul

videos.title: Reportage Grand Rendez-Vous Cyclistes de Charlevoix  20170812

gear_mentions.text: Rider competed successfully on a hardtail bike, noting it's nice and light, quick on the climbs with good acceleration out of corners.

race_day_tips.text: Focus on riding a steady race rather than trying to match others' tactics, especially if you know your competitors have a strong finishing kick.

additional_quotes.text: There was a lot of traffic though. It was a little bit short, few too many people on the course like we were lapping some people three different times.

rider_intel.search_text: Riders describe Granfondo Charlevoix as an excellent course with good flow that maintains its character despite course changes from previous years. The race features enough climbing that lightweight hardtail bikes perform well, offering quick acceleration on climbs and out of corners. Traffic can be a concern with multiple rider categories on course, leading to faster riders lapping others three times during the event. The course is considered short enough that traffic density becomes noticeable. Riders praise the race organization and consider it one of the favorite courses in Canada. The event maintains good flow throughout despite the traffic challenges, and experienced riders recommend focusing on steady pacing rather than reactive racing tactics.
